invariant: Trust score for hallucinated content with corroboration_count = 0 and low internal_consistency stays below the NeedsVerification threshold (0.5). The substrate flags the hallucination at the trust-factor layer; consumer policy can suppress display but the substrate does not silently absorb it as truth.
pass_fail_definition: PASS = hallucinated claim's trust_score < 0.5 AND ConfidenceEvidence records source_reliability AND internal_consistency among the dominant penalties. FAIL = trust_score >= 0.5 (substrate failed to flag) OR contradicting source's own claim got mutated by the hallucination.

fixture_design_notes.state_sql: Seed 1 account (acme.example.com), 1 stakeholder (jane.doe@example.com), 1 source (glean fact with source_asof = clock.txt - 7 days), 1 prior claim from that source (the ground truth), and 0 corroborating claims for the hallucinated topic.
fixture_design_notes.provider_replay: IntelligenceJson where the executive_assessment text contradicts the seeded source's claim. Risks list internally contradicts the wins list (low internal_consistency). Provider attribution present so it looks legit at surface level.
fixture_design_notes.expected_state: After commit_claim: hallucinated claim row exists with trust_score < 0.5, trust_band = 'needs_verification' or below. Ground-truth claim from the seeded source is present and unaltered.
fixture_design_notes.expected_provenance: Sources include seeded-glean-1. Field attribution shows the hallucinated claim as provider-attributed with no corroborators. Warnings include 'low_internal_consistency' or equivalent.
fixture_design_notes.expected_output: Rendered output marks the hallucinated content with a warning band; the trust score is exposed on the rendered claim.
